purpose: An adapter module that wraps an external REST API (OpenWeatherMap-style weather service) and exposes a simplified, typed interface for downstream modules. Handles authentication header injection, response normalization, and rate-limit awareness.

sidecar_specification.purpose: External weather API adapter with auth injection and response normalization

agent_usage.when_to_use: When downstream modules need weather data without managing external API keys or response formats
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: When direct external API calls are preferred for latency or when the upstream API is unavailable

failure_modes.recovery: Back off and retry with exponential delay

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not call hosts outside allowed list

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use native fetch. Read API key from process.env.WEATHER_API_KEY. Normalize OpenWeatherMap responses to a flat { temp_c, humidity, wind_speed, condition } object.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: OpenWeatherMap returns Kelvin by default. Ensure conversion to Celsius. Handle 404 for invalid coordinates gracefully.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Create a WeatherClient class with fetchCurrent(lat, lon) and fetchForecast(lat, lon). Implement a simple in-memory request deduplication cache (5s TTL).
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do NOT install axios or node-fetch — use native fetch. Do NOT store the API key in source code.
